title: [lora_l2.c:66-67] static rx_thread_data and rx_stack preclude multiple instances
description:
Category not checked: Module instantiation assumptions

Bug found: lora_l2.c uses static global state:
- Line 65: static K_THREAD_STACK_DEFINE(rx_stack, ...)
- Line 66: static struct k_thread rx_thread_data
- Line 69: static K_MUTEX_DEFINE(lora_mutex)
- Line 74: static K_MUTEX_DEFINE(tx_buf_mutex)
- Line 81: static uint8_t tx_buf[...]
- Line 94-107: static struct { ... } lora_state

This design allows only ONE LoRa radio instance per system. If a board has multiple LoRa radios (rare but possible with expansion boards), only one can be used.

The LORA_DEV macro at line 62 hardcodes DT_ALIAS(lora0) - there's no lora1 support.

This is a deliberate design choice (single-radio assumption) but undocumented. A future user trying to add multi-radio support would need to refactor all static state into per-instance structures.

Impact: P4 - Architectural limitation, not a bug but undocumented constraint.
close_reason: Fixed: Added architectural documentation explaining single-instance design is deliberate. Lists what multi-radio support would require and notes most boards have one radio.
